items.title: CVE-2026-35616: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Pre-Auth API Bypass Actively Exploited (CVSS 9.1)
items.summary: Fortinet patched a critical pre-authentication API access bypass in FortiClient EMS (CVSS 9.1) that allows unauthenticated attackers to escalate privileges. Active exploitation confirmed in the wild. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on April 6, 2026, with a federal patch deadline of April 9.
items.why: Pre-auth CVSS 9.1 flaw actively exploited — Fortinet products are a top ransomware initial-access vector. The federal patch window has closed, making non-patched enterprise deployments high-value targets right now.

items.title: CVE-2026-0257: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass Under Active Exploitation, CISA KEV Added
items.summary: An authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect portal and gateway allows unauthorized VPN tunnel establishment. Rapid7 confirmed active exploitation across multiple customers beginning May 17, 2026. CISA added it to KEV with a June 1 federal patch deadline.
items.why: GlobalProtect is one of the most widely deployed enterprise VPN solutions. An auth bypass here hands attackers direct network access — the June 1 federal deadline has passed, making unpatched orgs sitting targets.
items.source: The Hacker News / Rapid7 / CISA KEV

items.title: Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Deploys 6 New RAT Variants Across 5 Countries Amid Regional Conflict
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 is tracking an Iranian APT group dubbed Screening Serpens that deployed two novel malware families — MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 — across targets in up to five countries. Six distinct RAT variants were identified since February 2026, with campaign tempo escalating after the start of regional conflict.
items.why: Geopolitically-motivated espionage with novel, freshly-built RAT families means low AV detection rates. Escalating ops tied to active regional conflict signals sustained targeting of government, defense, and critical infrastructure sectors.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours, Targeting 9 Eastern European Defense and Diplomatic Entities
items.summary: Russian state-sponsored APT28 (Fancy Bear) launched a concentrated 72-hour spear-phishing campaign (Jan 28–30, 2026) exploiting CVE-2026-21509 within 24 hours of public disclosure. Targets spanned defense ministries (40%), transportation/logistics (35%), and diplomatic entities (25%) across nine Eastern European nations. Cloud-based C2 infrastructure used for stealth.
items.why: A 24-hour weaponization window against NATO-adjacent targets highlights that patch SLAs measured in days are too slow. Cloud-based C2 abuses trusted services to bypass perimeter defenses — a tradecraft trend defenders must account for.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Attackers Hand Off Access in 22 Seconds; LLM-Querying Malware (PROMPTFLUX) Emerges
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report, drawn from 450,000 hours of incident response, reveals attackers are internally handing off compromised access in as little as 22 seconds. New malware families PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL query live LLMs mid-execution to dynamically generate evasion commands. Vulnerability exploitation leads initial access at 32% of all intrusions.
items.why: 22-second lateral movement hand-offs make human-speed SOC response insufficient — automated detection and response are now mandatory. LLM-querying malware is a new evasion class that signature and heuristic tools cannot reliably catch.
